title: Current version fails on `windows-gnu` builds due to `sha1-asm`
author: RivenSkaye
body:
Due to the compiler instead of target check [in `Cargo.toml` on line 30](https://github.com/not-an-aardvark/lucky-commit/blob/0000000528d30e42901f5650a6597371380c815b/Cargo.toml#L30) the current build fails for any `*windows-gnu*` toolchain due to [`sha1-asm` explicitly checking the target OS](https://github.com/RustCrypto/asm-hashes/blob/8e805227e0ccee2de96c35809d9e5b2f1cb5f5f6/sha1/src/lib.rs#L15). It's a simple ~~single line~~ two line fix to replace. Patch is included at the bottom of this issue.

```
error: crate does not support Windows targets
  --> C:/msys64/home/Riven/.rust/.cargo\registry\src\index.crates.io-1949cf8c6b5b557f\sha1-asm-0.5.3\src\lib.rs:16:1
   |
16 | compile_error!("crate does not support Windows targets");
   | ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^

   Compiling qutex v0.2.6
error: could not compile `sha1-asm` (lib) due to 1 previous error
warning: build failed, waiting for other jobs to finish...
error: failed to compile `lucky_commit v2.2.4`, intermediate artifacts can be found at `C:\msys64\tmp\cargo-installB7DnCI`.
To reuse those artifacts with a future compilation, set the environment variable `CARGO_TARGET_DIR` to that path.
```

[winfix.patch](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19157785/winfix.patch)

title: Support creating commit hash that does not contain a specific char
author: hustcer
body:
Support creating a commit hash that does not contain a specific char or string, for example:
`luck_commit --not 1` will create a commit hash does not contain `1`
`luck_commit --not 13` will create a commit hash does not contain `13`

title: Finding 12 character prefix collision
author: kees
body:
Hi!

I tried to find a 12 character prefix collision but lucky_commit exhausted the search space. (This took about 7 hours on an RTX 3080.) If I'm reading lib.rs correctly, this is because "only" 48 bytes (a single dynamic block) are being mutated? I was thinking I could raise the search space from 48 to 96 to add another dynamic block but ended up getting stumped. Is it possible to expand the search space? Or add more characters to the whitespace set (like \r, \n, maybe \v and \f)?

title: rust 1.77.0 build failure
author: chenrui333
body:
👋 when [upgrading rust to 1.77.0](https://github.com/Homebrew/homebrew-core/pull/166865), we ran into some regression build failure as below:

```
error[E0310]: the parameter type `T` may not live long enough
    --> /Users/brew/Library/Caches/Homebrew/cargo_cache/registry/src/index.crates.io-6f17d22bba15001f/rustc-serialize-0.3.24/src/serialize.rs:1155:5
     |
1155 |     fn decode<D: Decoder>(d: &mut D) -> Result<Cow<'static, T>, D::Error> {
     |     ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
     |     |
     |     the parameter type `T` must be valid for the static lifetime...
     |     ...so that the type `T` will meet its required lifetime bounds...
     |
note: ...that is required by this bound
    --> /private/tmp/rust-20240322-5607-zxyuuj/rustc-1.77.0-src/library/alloc/src/borrow.rs:180:30
help: consider adding an explicit lifetime bound
     |
1151 | impl<'a, T: ?Sized + 'static> Decodable for Cow<'a, T>
     |                    +++++++++

   Compiling rand v0.4.6
   Compiling crossbeam-epoch v0.8.2
   Compiling miniz_oxide v0.4.4
   Compiling num-iter v0.1.42
For more information about this error, try `rustc --explain E0310`.
error: could not compile `rustc-serialize` (lib) due to 1 previous error
```

I think a release for [this commit](https://github.com/not-an-aardvark/lucky-commit/commit/00000006f95e4307a30492fd2bb02cc6872d9edf) would solve the issue.

title: how to debug why lucky_commit won't use my gpu

title: Assertion error when linked with intel-compute-runtime
author: mat813
body:
Using Arch Linux, today, lucky_commit fails, I was using the arch package, cargo installed it, and it fails all the same:

```
~/.cargo/bin/lucky_commit 0000000                                      
/usr/include/c++/12.2.0/bits/stl_vector.h:1142: std::vector<_Tp, _Alloc>::const_reference std::vector<_Tp, _Alloc>::operator[](size_type) const [with _Tp = NEO::ArgTypeMetadataExtended; _Alloc = std::allocator<NEO::ArgTypeMetadataExtended>; const_reference = const NEO::ArgTypeMetadataExtended&; size_type = long unsigned int]: Assertion '__n < this->size()' failed.
[1]    92025 IOT instruction (core dumped)  ~/.cargo/bin/lucky_commit 0000000
```

This is using an intel graphics card with intel-compute-runtime installed.

If I remove intel-compute-runtime, it works, but, well, it is slooowww :disappointed:

title: Commit signature not detected
author: jmah
body:
My commit signing flow with X509 signatures results in the commit object containing:

```
gpgsig -----BEGIN SIGNED MESSAGE-----
 …
 -----END SIGNED MESSAGE-----
```

This is not detected by `lucky-commit` since it's explicitly looking for `-----END PGP SIGNATURE-----`.

title: Test
author: float3
body: which commit will GitHub link to when I do 0000000

title: doing a specific hash is somehow MUCH slower than the default
author: JacksonChen666
body:
i was playing around a bit with [hyperfine](https://github.com/sharkdp/hyperfine), stumbled upon an option called `--parameter-scan`, chose a random max number (7 digits) and a min number of `9371543` based on the max number with a bit of subtraction, and ran the thing

somehow, lucky_commit was doing a hash of `9371543` around 6 times slower than `0000000` (assumed to be the default of lucky_commit)

i decided to test if it was just weird thing. nope, it is consistently slower.

<details><summary>device details</summary>

- m1 mac (13 inch 2020, not pro or max or ultra)
- running macos 12.3 (21E230)
- architecture of program (or the `file` command): `/Users/jackson/.cargo/bin/lucky_commit: Mach-O 64-bit executable arm64`
- 8gb of memory

</details>

<details><summary>benchmark details</summary>

- gpu was used (99% usage basically)
- command: `hyperfine --warmup 10 --export-json ../benchmark.json --runs 25 "lucky_commit 9371543" "lucky_commit 0000000"`
- output (uncolored):
```
Benchmark 1: lucky_commit 9371543
  Time (mean ± σ):     917.1 ms ±   5.9 ms    [User: 61.1 ms, System: 83.7 ms]
  Range (min … max):   908.7 ms … 931.2 ms    25 runs
 
Benchmark 2: lucky_commit 0000000
  Time (mean ± σ):     155.9 ms ±   3.2 ms    [User: 27.2 ms, System: 23.4 ms]
  Range (min … max):   148.7 ms … 161.1 ms    25 runs
 
Summary
  'lucky_commit 0000000' ran
    5.88 ± 0.13 times faster than 'lucky_commit 9371543'
```
- git repo (last state i left it in): [repo.bundle.zip](https://github.com/not-an-aardvark/lucky-commit/files/8352208/repo.bundle.zip)
  - commits were not signed
  - 1 commit
  - [how to "use"](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/39940351/export-github-repository#39940468)
- raw benchmark data (json): [benchmark.json.zip](https://github.com/not-an-aardvark/lucky-commit/files/8352233/benchmark.json.zip)
- git repo location: the desktop
  - trying to run `lucky_commit` in `/tmp` on macos is actually somehow slower, so avoid that

</details>

title: Runtime crash on Windows
author: kasthack
body:
It seems that Rust chooses the wrong type for `BASE_PADDING_SPECIFIER_ARG` argument for some reason and it causes lucky_commit to crash.

NVIDIA stack trace:
```
> lucky_commit --benchmark

thread 'main' panicked at 'called `Result::unwrap()` on an `Err` value:

################################ OPENCL ERROR ###############################

Error executing function: clSetKernelArg("scatter_padding_and_find_match")

Status error code: CL_INVALID_ARG_SIZE (-51)

Please visit the following url for more information:

https://www.khronos.org/registry/cl/sdk/1.2/docs/man/xhtml/clSetKernelArg.html#errors

#############################################################################
', C:\files\source\github\not-an-aardvark\lucky-commit\src/lib.rs:301:43
note: run with `RUST_BACKTRACE=1` environment variable to display a backtrace
```

Intel stack trace:

```
> lucky_commit --benchmark

thread 'main' panicked at 'called `Result::unwrap()` on an `Err` value: Kernel argument type mismatch. The argument named: 'base_padding_specifier' at index: [3] should be a 'ulong' (ArgType { base_type: Ulong, cardinality: One, is_ptr: false }).', C:\files\source\github\not-an-aardvark\lucky-commit\src/lib.rs:301:43
```

Environment:

````
PS C:\Windows\system32> cargo --version
cargo 1.55.0 (32da73ab1 2021-08-23)
PS C:\Windows\system32> rustc --version
rustc 1.55.0 (c8dfcfe04 2021-09-06)

Windows 10 Pro x64 build 19043.1237
````

I've fixed the issue in #19.

title: Docker image
author: bokub
body:
Would you consider releasing a Docker image for people who don't have rust installed?

For example this is the recommended way to use Go Swagger: https://goswagger.io/install.html

You just pull a Docker image, then create an alias that uses the docker image, instead of installing or compiling a binary.

Thanks!

title: Add console output for successful execution
author: Marcono1234
body:
Currently when lucky-commit succeeds there is no output in the console. This makes you wonder whether it succeeded (but on the other hand creates a surprise effect when you then run `git log`).

What do you think about printing a message to console on success? That message could for example contain the new commit hash.

title: Segmentation fault in `sha1_compress()`
author: Marcono1234
body:
Hello,
I am currently trying to build and run this project on Windows 10, but I am experiencing a segmentation fault in `sha1_compress()`.
I am neither familiar with Rust nor with building and debugging of native applications, so maybe I made a mistake during some of the steps, any hints are appreciated.

1. Initially running `cargo build --release --no-default-features` failed compiling `sha1-asm` with an error similar to what is described in https://github.com/RustCrypto/asm-hashes/issues/17. Therefore I uninstalled Rust with MSVC ABI and reinstalled it with GNU ABI.
2. Then the build failed with
    > ```
    > error: failed to run custom build command for `sha1-asm v0.4.4`
    > ...
    > error occurred: Failed to find tool. Is `gcc.exe` installed? (see https://github.com/alexcrichton/cc-rs#compile-time-requirements for help)
    > ```
3. I followed that link, installed MSYS2 MinGW with `gcc`.
4. The build still failed with the same error so I added `C:\msys64\mingw64\bin` (containing `gcc`) to the `PATH` environment variable.
5. At some point I also got `cannot find -lOpenCL`, but that seems to be resolved now after I installed the NVIDIA Cuda Toolkit (and tinkered with the `.cargo/config` file?).
6. `cargo build --release` and `cargo install lucky_commit --locked` (for commit 00000005795b7f4ecb1ded7b663e288c2290fa66) both now succeed.
7. Running `lucky_commit.exe` within my local clone of this repository exits without any feedback.
The exit code is -1073741819 (0xC0000005).
Running it with too many arguments, e.g. `lucky_commit.exe a b` works correctly, it prints the usage help and has exit code 1.
8. Using `gdb.exe lucky_commit.exe` from the MSYS terminal with `export RUST_BACKTRACE=full` shows:
    ```
    Thread 1 received signal SIGSEGV, Segmentation fault.
    0x00007ff63e52bc31 in sha1_compress ()
    (gdb) bt
    #0  0x00007ff63e52bc31 in sha1_compress ()
    #1  0x00007ff9979747b1 in ntdll!RtlFreeHeap ()
       from C:\WINDOWS\SYSTEM32\ntdll.dll
    #2  0x00007ff63e4bb68c in lucky_commit::HashSearchWorker::search ()
    #3  0x00007ff63e4a3106 in lucky_commit::run_lucky_commit ()
    #4  0x00007ff63e4a1d20 in lucky_commit::main ()
    #5  0x00007ff63e4daea6 in std::sys_common::backtrace::__rust_begin_short_backtrace ()
    #6  0x00007ff63e4a7b87 in main ()
    ```

Do you have an idea what I might be doing wrong, or do you need more information?

title: error on build
author: c-z-chen
body:
```
➜  lucky-commit git:(C) make                       
gcc -Wall -std=c99 -pedantic -O3 -DNDEBUG -flto -pthread -o lucky-commit lucky-commit.c -I/usr/local/opt/openssl/include/ -lcrypto -lm -lz
lucky-commit.c: In function ‘luckyCommit’:
lucky-commit.c:386:15: error: assignment of read-only location ‘params[i]’
  386 |     params[i] = (struct HashSearchParams){
      |               ^
make: *** [Makefile:2: default] Error 1
```

I do not understand C and therefore do not under stand this.... the rust version does not function on my vps too... it was because of some `linking with cc failed: exit status: 1` issue...

as for the node.js version, it says something like this:

title: Failed to create git object file: Not a directory
author: LoganDark
body:
```
LoganDark@base ~/n/backend> lucky_commit
thread 'main' panicked at 'Failed to create git object file: Error { repr: Os { code: 20, message: "Not a directory" } }', src/libcore/result.rs:906:4
note: Run with `RUST_BACKTRACE=1` for a backtrace.
```

I'm running it again right now, I'm going to post the backtrace here when it's done.
